steps.name: Phase 0: Identify Functions to Test
steps.description: Analyze target functions and create prioritized testing list
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.claude/agents/coverage-phase-0.md. Run exclusively the coverage-phase-0 agent using the Task tool. This phase will identify all public functions in target contracts (excluding Admin, Managers, and Doomsday targets), analyze their dependencies, and create a prioritized ordered list in magic/testing_priority.md.

steps.name: Check that testing_priority.md exists
steps.description: Confirms that the previous step generated a testing_priority.md file

steps.name: Phase 1: Identify Contracts to Cover
steps.description: Determine which contracts need full coverage by analyzing Setup.sol and contract dependencies
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.claude/agents/coverage-phase-1.md. Run exclusively the coverage-phase-1 agent using the Task tool. This phase will identify all contracts deployed in Setup.sol (excluding mocks), run forge build with build-info, use sol-expand to extract context, and create magic/coverage/contracts-to-cover.md with an exhaustive list of contracts that need coverage.

steps.name: Check that contracts-to-cover.md exists
steps.description: Confirms that the previous step generated a contracts-to-cover.md file

steps.name: Phase 2: Setup Testing - Implement Unit Tests
steps.description: Implement unit tests for all functions in testing_priority.md and verify Setup.sol configuration
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.claude/agents/coverage-phase-2.md. Run exclusively the coverage-phase-2 agent using the Task tool. This phase will: 1) Move admin-only functions to AdminTargets.sol, 2) Implement unit tests in CryticToFoundry.sol for all functions in testing_priority.md (in order), 3) Fix Setup.sol configuration and permissions issues, 4) Document any justified reverting handlers in magic/reverting_handlers.md, and 5) Generate magic/test-notes.md summarizing the test implementation.

steps.name: Phase 3: Initial Coverage Assessment
steps.description: Run Echidna to establish baseline coverage and identify handlers missing coverage
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.claude/agents/coverage-phase-3.md. Run exclusively the coverage-phase-3 agent using the Task tool. This phase will: 1) Verify Echidna runs successfully with test-limit 10000 and check for specific success messages, 2) Run Echidna for 30 minutes to generate coverage report, 3) Read the latest covered.<timestamp>.txt file using reading-coverage.md guidelines, 4) Verify all unit-tested functions show coverage, and 5) Create magic/coverage/handlers_missing_covg.md listing functions from contracts-to-cover.md that lack coverage.

steps.name: Rerun prevoius phase if handlers_missing_covg.md exists
steps.description: Confirms if the previous step generated a handlers_missing_covg.md file and reruns the phase of generating unit tests if so

steps.name: Phase 4: Create Clamped Handlers
steps.description: Implement clamped handlers to improve coverage for functions in handlers_missing_covg.md
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.claude/agents/coverage-phase-4.md. Run exclusively the coverage-phase-4 agent using the Task tool. This phase will: 1) Review handlers_missing_covg.md to identify which functions need clamping, 2) Create dictionary_entries.md with possible Echidna dictionary values, 3) Implement clamped handlers (with _clamped postfix) following all 7 clamping rules, 4) Only add clamped handlers to files in recon/targets/ directory above the CUSTOM TARGET FUNCTIONS header, and 5) Run Echidna for 2 hours to validate the clamped handlers improve coverage.

steps.name: Phase 5: Coverage Loop Assessment
steps.description: Assess coverage and determine if more iterations are needed
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.claude/agents/coverage-phase-5.md. Run exclusively the coverage-phase-5 agent using the Task tool. This phase will: 1) Read the latest coverage report using reading-coverage.md guidelines, 2) If remaining-uncovered.md doesn't exist, create it with all uncovered functions from contracts-to-cover.md, 3) If remaining-uncovered.md exists, update it by removing now-covered functions, 4) If all functions are covered, create COVERAGE_COMPLETE.md, otherwise create COVERAGE_NEEDS_ITERATION.md.

steps.name: Rerun previous phase if remaining-uncovered.md exists
steps.description: Confirms if the previous step generated a remaining-uncovered.md file and reruns the phase of generating unit tests if so

steps.name: Run Linting Agent
steps.description: Runs the linting agent to determine if the suite was setup according to best practices
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.claude/agents/chimera-test-linter.md. Exclusively run the coverage-phase-5 agent using the Task tool.
